---
name: context-instructions
description: >-
  Generate/refresh the Enterprise Context section in .github/copilot-instructions.md. INSTRUCTIONS SKILL. USE FOR: completing context-wizard, refreshing after MCP/docs/distill changes, repairing this section's merge. DO NOT USE FOR: .mcp.json changes, MCP discovery/auth, source docs, commits, deployment. INVOKES: file tools, session_state, ask_user for manual-edit confirmation.
license: MIT
metadata:
  version: 0.0.1
  user-invocable: true
---

Maintain one `## Enterprise Context` section in `.github/copilot-instructions.md`; modify no other files. Dry-run uses fixtures; describe only.

## Inputs
- `.mcp.json`: `mcpServers`; document every server name, coverage, key tools, and usage; label unknown coverage.
- `tagged_doc_sources` from `context-docs`: `[{category,platform,location,tag,url?,notes?}]`.
- `distilled_intent` from docs/history/distill: `{intent[],constraints[],autonomy[],topology[],history_observations?}`.
- Optional: `healthcheck_results`, `.github/intent-changelog.md`.

## Procedure
1. Load contracts; if absent/malformed, report the gap in output and generate only supported sections.
2. Draft `## Enterprise Context` with marker `<!-- generated by context-instructions -->`.
3. Include MCP table (name, coverage, tools/use, health), intent, regulatory constraints, autonomy table `Action|Level|Notes` (`PROCEED`, `ALWAYS ASK`, `NEVER`), topology, docs, workflows, skills/agents. If changelog exists add footer: `> Intent changelog: [.github/intent-changelog.md](intent-changelog.md) | Last updated: {date}`.
4. Merge safely: create if absent; append if no Enterprise Context; replace through next same-level `## ` or EOF. Preserve all else.
5. If replacing and the section lacks the marker or looks customized, ask: "The Enterprise Context section appears to have manual edits. Regenerating will replace them. Proceed?" Declined/cancelled/unavailable: report skipped; leave unchanged.
6. Report outcome or write failure.

## Boundary
Never edit `.mcp.json`, docs, changelogs, code, commits, deployments, or auth config.
